description: Single filer, Schedule C with full Part II expenses ($27,000) plus home office ($3,000), no W-2s, $10,000 in 2025 estimated tax payments. Regression lock for the blocker-1 fix: proves the engine subtracts Schedule C expenses (so AGI is ~$83k, NOT ~$120k) and that SE tax plus the 1/2 SE tax adjustment flow through. QBI deduction (Section 199A) applies: min(20% of $90k QBI, 20% of $67,891.70 TI) = $13,578.34.
authority: tenforty evaluate_return for self-employment tax, 1/2 SE tax adjustment, and federal income tax
IRS 2025 Schedule SE: SE tax = net profit * 0.9235 * 0.153 when net SE earnings < $176,100 SS wage base
IRS Form 8995: QBI deduction = min(20% of total QBI, 20% of TI before QBI)

hand_check.se_tax_calc: 90000 * 0.9235 * 0.153 = 12717.03 (tenforty produces 12716.60, within $0.50 due to intermediate rounding)
hand_check.agi_calc: 90000 (SE net profit) - 6358.30 (1/2 SE tax) = 83641.70 — THIS IS THE REGRESSION LOCK. If AGI approaches 120000, the engine has lost Schedule C expenses.
